Title: King of Sparta
Biography: Led the legendary stand of 300 Spartans at the Battle of Thermopylae against the Persian invasion.

Title: French military leader and saint
Biography: Peasant girl who led French armies to key victories during the Hundred Years War before being burned at the stake.

Title: Last active pharaoh of Egypt
Biography: The last ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt; allied with Julius Caesar and later Mark Antony.

Title: King of Macedon
Biography: Conquered the Persian Empire and most of the known world by the age of thirty.

Title: Theologian and philosopher
Biography: Dominican friar and author of the Summa Theologica; central figure in medieval scholastic thought.

Title: Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher
Biography: Philosopher-emperor whose private journal Meditations remains a foundational Stoic text.

Title: Founder of the Mongol Empire
Biography: United the Mongol tribes and founded the largest contiguous land empire in history.

Title: Sultan of Egypt and Syria
Biography: First sultan of the Ayyubid dynasty; recaptured Jerusalem from the Crusaders in 1187.

Title: Persian polymath
Biography: Author of The Canon of Medicine which remained a standard medical textbook in Europe for centuries.

Title: Empress of Russia
Biography: Russia's longest-ruling female leader; presided over an era of expansion and Enlightenment reforms.

Title: Roman dictator and general
Biography: Transformed the Roman Republic through his military conquests and political reforms before his assassination.

Title: Jewish philosopher and physician
Biography: Medieval rabbi whose Mishneh Torah and Guide for the Perplexed remain central to Jewish thought.

Title: Carthaginian general
Biography: Crossed the Alps with war elephants to invade Italy during the Second Punic War.

Title: Mauryan emperor
Biography: Indian emperor who spread Buddhism across Asia after the brutal Kalinga War.

Title: Queen of the Iceni
Biography: Celtic warrior queen who led an uprising against Roman occupation of Britain.

Title: Chinese military strategist
Biography: Ancient Chinese general whose treatise The Art of War remains influential in military and business strategy.

Title: King of the Franks
Biography: United much of western Europe and was crowned Emperor of the Romans in 800.

Title: Pharaoh of Egypt
Biography: Fifth pharaoh of the Eighteenth Dynasty; one of the most successful pharaohs and a rare female ruler.

Title: Roman emperor
Biography: First Roman emperor to convert to Christianity; founded Constantinople as the new imperial capital.

Title: Queen of France and England
Biography: One of the wealthiest and most powerful women in twelfth-century Europe; mother of Richard the Lionheart.

Title: Theologian and bishop
Biography: Author of Confessions and City of God; shaped Western Christianity for over a millennium.

Title: Sultan of the Ottoman Empire
Biography: Presided over the Ottoman Empire at its peak; reformed legal systems still known as Kanuni.

Title: Mali emperor
Biography: Ruler of the Mali Empire; his pilgrimage to Mecca made him famous as the richest person in history.

Title: Empress regnant of China
Biography: Only woman to officially rule China as emperor in her own right; founded the short-lived Zhou dynasty.

Title: Athenian statesman
Biography: Led Athens during its golden age; commissioned the Parthenon and championed democracy.

Title: Queen of the Massagetae
Biography: Defeated and killed Cyrus the Great of Persia in battle in 530 BC.

Title: Mughal emperor
Biography: Expanded the Mughal Empire and pursued religious tolerance across his diverse domain.

Title: Roman emperor
Biography: Expanded the Roman Empire to its greatest territorial extent; remembered as one of the Five Good Emperors.

Title: Arab military commander
Biography: Undefeated general who led the early Muslim conquests of Mesopotamia and the Levant.

Title: Ethiopian emperor
Biography: Zagwe king who commissioned the rock-hewn churches that still bear his name.
